Name: Wat Phra Kaew
description: The Emerald Buddha temple, a must-see royal landmark.

Name: Thip Samai Pad Thai
description: The most famous Pad Thai in Bangkok with "Superb" recipe.

Name: Wat Arun
description: The stunning Riverside "Temple of Dawn" with porcelain decor.

Name: Jay Fai
description: Legendary Michelin-starred Crab Omelette by a local icon.

Name: Museum Siam
description: Interactive museum explaining the history of "Thainess".

Name: Blue Whale Cafe
description: Famous blue butterfly pea latte in a cozy riverside house.

Name: Yaowarat Road
description: The vibrant heart of Chinatown, best for night street food.

Name: Nai Ek Roll Noodle
description: Famous crispy pork and peppery rolled noodles in Chinatown.

Name: Wat Saket
description: The Golden Mount with a panoramic view of Old Bangkok.

Name: Jeh O Chula
description: Late-night spicy MAMA Tom Yum noodles that worth the wait.

Name: Jim Thompson House
description: A beautiful complex of traditional Thai teak houses.

Name: Sorn
description: Elite Southern Thai cuisine using rare local ingredients.

Name: ICONSIAM
description: World-class mall with "SookSiam" indoor floating market.

Name: Here Hai
description: Insane Garlic Crab Meat and fried rice, Michelin Bib Gourmand.

Name: Chatuchak Market
description: The world’s largest weekend market for everything you need.

Name: Pe Aor Tom Yum
description: Creamy Tom Yum Kung with giant river prawns.

Name: Erawan Shrine
description: Famous Hindu shrine in the city center known for good luck.

Name: Gaggan Anand
description: Progressive Indian cuisine voted best in Asia many times.

Name: Benjakitti Park
description: Modern green lung with skywalks and great skyline views.

Name: Somtum Der
description: Authentic Isan (Northeast) Thai food in a trendy setting.

Name: Pak Khlong Talat
description: Bangkok's biggest 24-hour flower and vegetable market.

Name: Le Du
description: Modern Thai seasonal dishes, 1 Michelin Star & Asia's No.1.

Name: Wat Pho
description: Home to the giant Reclining Buddha and traditional massage.

Name: Taling Pling
description: Classic Thai home cooking in a beautiful garden house.

Name: Ong Ang Canal
description: Renovated canal with street art and weekend night market.

Name: Polo Fried Chicken
description: Legendary fried chicken with tons of crispy golden garlic.

Name: Mahanakhon Skywalk
description: Thailand's highest observation deck with a glass tray floor.

Name: Kor Panich
description: The best Mango Sticky Rice using a 90-year-old recipe.

Name: Baan Silapin
description: Artist's House with traditional Thai puppet shows by canal.

Name: Wattana Panich
description: 40-year-old giant pot of beef stew simmered for decades.

Name: Kuay Jab Mr. Joe
description: A legendary Michelin Bib Gourmand roll noodle shop on Thanon Chan, open for over 50 years. Famous for crispy pork and flavorful pork innards broth with strong black pepper.

Name: 25 Degrees Bangkok
description: An iconic American-style burger bar in Sathorn, known for thick juicy patties on brioche buns. A long-standing Bangkok favorite with a retro diner vibe.

Name: Tuang Dim Sum
description: A beloved old-school dim sum shop on Charoen Krung, famous for thin-skinned har gao, juicy shrimp shumai, and crispy taro puffs. Opens early morning.

Name: Moo Krob Nai Sai
description: A well-known crispy pork shop in Pracha Chuen area serving authentic Thai-Chinese style crispy pork belly with rice. Simple, satisfying, and budget-friendly.

Name: Khao Tom Pla Kimpo
description: A traditional Thai rice porridge shop specializing in fish congee with silky smooth broth, fish balls, and tofu. A comforting late-night or early morning meal.

Name: Heng Chun Seng
description: A classic Thai-Chinese hot pot restaurant serving old-style charcoal steamboat with assorted offal, meatballs, and greens. Authentic old Bangkok dining experience.

Name: Kai Thot Che Ki Soi Polo
description: A legendary Polo area restaurant famous for golden crispy fried chicken paired with papaya salad and spicy Isaan dishes. A must-try Bangkok street food institution.

Name: Baan Phueng Chom
description: A riverside Thai restaurant known for upscale-casual Thai cuisine including noodle glass salads, wagyu beef, and creative Thai fusion dishes in a relaxed setting.

Name: Boon Tong Kiat Singapore Chicken
description: Bangkok's most famous Singaporean Hainanese chicken rice, serving silky steamed chicken with fragrant oily rice, ginger sauce, and dark soy. Always crowded.

Name: Ann Kuay Teow Kua Gai
description: A popular stir-fried noodle shop serving Thai-style wok-charred flat noodles with chicken, egg, and crispy pork. Known for the perfect wok hei smoky flavor.

Name: SEA LIFE Bangkok Ocean World
description: Bangkok's largest indoor aquarium located inside Siam Paragon. Features underwater tunnels, shark tanks, penguin zones, and interactive marine exhibits. Perfect for families and couples.

Name: Asiatique The Riverfront
description: A large open-air riverside night market and entertainment complex on the Chao Phraya River. Features shopping, dining, live shows, and a giant Ferris wheel. Best visited at night.

Name: Klong Bang Luang Floating Market
description: A hidden old Bangkok canal community famous for traditional long-tail boat rides, local art galleries, and a peaceful glimpse into vintage Bangkok life.

Name: Bangkok Art and Culture
description: Bangkok's leading contemporary art museum near Siam BTS. A multi-floor circular gallery hosting rotating exhibitions of Thai and international art, photography, and installations. Free entry.

Name: Lumpini Park
description: Bangkok's most beloved urban green space in the heart of the city. Popular for morning exercise, paddle boating, and sunset walks. Home to monitor lizards and a relaxing city escape.

Name: Wat Phra Kaew
description: Thailand's most sacred temple and royal palace complex. Home to the revered Emerald Buddha statue. A must-visit icon representing Thai history, architecture, and spirituality.

Name: Yaowarat
description: Bangkok's vibrant Chinatown packed with neon signs, street food stalls, gold shops, and Chinese-Thai heritage. Best experienced at night when the energy peaks.

Name: Wang Lang Pier
description: A lively riverside pier on the Thonburi side serving as a gateway to local markets and Siriraj Hospital area. Popular for cheap eats and catching Chao Phraya Express Boats.

Name: Wang Lang Market
description: A dense and chaotic local street market near Wang Lang pier. Famous for affordable Thai street food, clothing, and everyday goods. Beloved by locals and adventurous tourists alike.

Name: Talat Noi
description: One of Bangkok's oldest riverside neighborhoods turned creative district. Known for colorful street art murals, old Chinese shophouses, indie cafes, and a thriving art scene.

Name: Prachak Roasted Duck
description: Famous roasted duck in Chinatown with crispy skin and rich sauce

Name: Krua Sa Ros Jad
description: Authentic Thai restaurant with bold and spicy flavors loved by locals

Name: On Lok Yun
description: Vintage breakfast spot serving kaya toast eggs and Thai coffee

Name: Si Morakot
description: Crispy pork rice with signature green gravy sauce

Name: Rung Rueang Pork Noodle
description: Michelin recommended pork noodle soup with rich broth

Name: Nhong Rimklong
description: Street food beside railway known for intense wok flavor

Name: Ladmayom Floating Market
description: Authentic floating market with local food and less tourists

Name: Kwan Riam Floating Market
description: Cultural floating market connecting two temples by boat

Name: Taling Chan Floating Market
description: Small riverside market with seafood and boat tours

Name: Wang Lang Market
description: Popular market with trendy snacks and clothes near hospital

Name: Chao Phraya Sky Park
description: Sky park on old bridge with river view and sunset vibes

Name: Forest Learning Center
description: Urban forest learning space focused on sustainability

Name: Harajuku Thailand
description: Japanese style street with shops and cosplay atmosphere

Name: LHONG 1919
description: Historic Chinese riverside mansion turned into cultural space

Name: Wat Saket
description: Golden Mount temple with panoramic Bangkok city view

Name: Santi Chai Prakan Park
description: Riverside park with sunset views and live performances

Name: Princess Mother Memorial Park
description: Peaceful park with traditional Thai houses and museum

Name: the COMMONS Saladaeng
description: Modern lifestyle space with cafes and creative dining spots

Name: Dusit Central Park Sky Garden
description: Luxury sky garden with panoramic city skyline

Name: Thaworawatthu Building
description: Historic building showcasing classic Bangkok architecture
